question: Meta's sustainability strategy encompasses four major environmental areas: Net Zero, Water, Biodiversity, and Circularity. Synthesize the headline target, the key metric, and one specific example for each area from the report.
answers: Net Zero: Maintained net zero in global operations since 2020, reduced operational emissions by 94% from 2017; 10,000+ MW renewable energy portfolio. Example: market-based emissions 39% smaller than location-based through renewable procurement (page 11). Water: Water positive by 2030 restoring 200% in high-stress and 100% in medium-stress areas. In 2022, restored 621 million gallons to stressed regions (page 38). Biodiversity: Preserved and planned over 3,400 acres of native habitat across 20 North American data centers, representing 58% of data center fleet footprint (page 27). Circularity: Prioritizes post-consumer recycled plastics and recycled metal in hardware; reused components showing excellent results with oldest racks at two years of age performing well (page 24).

Four pillars: (1) Net Zero — 94% reduction from 2017, 10,000+ MW renewables; (2) Water Positive by 2030 — 200%/100% restoration targets, 621M gallons restored in 2022; (3) Biodiversity — 3,400+ acres preserved across 20 data centers (58% of footprint); (4) Circularity — PCR plastics, recycled metals, reused components performing well at 2 years.
